patches.rationale: WIN-019.c6 curmudgeon review: the '0.4% tolerance implied below' is the full-width of the ±0.2% half-width tolerance — a confusing distinction that carries no analytical weight. Per curmudgeon recommendation, drop the trailing clause so the bullet reads cleanly: 'this is a derived rather than a primitive metric' without the stale half-width/full-width disambiguation.
